100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 24, 1926, the British Empire celebrated Empire Day with grand parades and festivities across its dominions, highlighting the unity and strength of the Commonwealth. In the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition intensified as various states grappled with calls for repeal amid rising crime and public dissent. Meanwhile, tensions escalated in the Middle East as the situation in Palestine grew increasingly volatile, prompting concerns from international leaders.
Updated: Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at 08:55:54 AM CDT
